ecologist.name: Aqib Farid
ecologist.title: Project Ecologist | Botanist | Bush Regenerator
ecologist.profile: Botanist and Ecologist with 4+ years of combined experience in ecological fieldwork, bush regeneration, flora surveys, threatened species conservation and environmental research. Skilled in identifying threatened flora, priority weeds and fauna habitat, conducting ecological assessments, and using GPS-enabled tablets and GIS to collect and manage field data. Passionate about delivering high-quality ecological outcomes while maintaining strong safety standards and effective client communication. Based in Central West NSW and committed to contributing to impactful conservation and restoration work.

strengths.title: NSW Flora & Fauna
strengths.text: Strong knowledge of native plants, threatened species & fauna habitat

strengths.title: Threatened Flora
strengths.text: Experienced in identifying and protecting threatened flora & key habitats

strengths.title: Priority Weeds
strengths.text: Identify and manage priority weeds to support habitat health

strengths.title: GPS & GIS
strengths.text: Skilled in field data collection using GPS & GIS platforms

strengths.title: Independent & Client
strengths.text: Able to work remotely, liaise with clients & deliver quality results

highlights: Conduct ecological restoration and bush regeneration across multiple council and private sites.
Identify and protect threatened flora including Grevillea juniperina and pencil orchids during works.
Undertake priority weed control (glyphosate, foliar spraying, cut-and-paint) to improve vegetation condition.
Collect native seed, propagate plants and deliver habitat restoration and erosion control works.
Collaborate with project leads to ensure works meet ecological and WHS standards.
Assisted in native plant collections and conservation programs at Taronga Zoo.
Identified native and threatened flora including Grevillea exalata and assisted with habitat restoration.
Monitored plant health and supported horticultural specialists with management of native collections.
Applied botanical knowledge to contribute to in-situ and ex-situ conservation outcomes.
Designed and implemented lab, greenhouse and field experiments on soil–plant–microbe interactions.
Evaluated plant responses to environmental stress across various soil and climatic conditions.
Analysed data using R and SAS to generate insights for sustainable plant management.
Produced comprehensive technical reports and presented findings to senior management and clients.
Applied molecular techniques (PCR, microscopy) to study plant health and disease resistance.
Conducted experiments on soil microbiota and plant stress tolerance for sustainable agriculture.
Assisted in field trials, data collection and lab analysis for multidisciplinary plant science projects.
Monitored vegetation response and recorded field data using GPS-enabled tablets and Survey123.
Co-authored a peer-reviewed publication in the Asian Soil Journal.

education.detail: Relevant Areas: Biotechnology, Molecular Biology, Environmental Applications, Research Methods

Key Subjects: Plant Ecology I & II, Biodiversity & Conservation, Plant Taxonomy, Plant Physiology, Environmental Biology, Plant Anatomy, Molecular Biology, Genetics, Mycology & Plant Pathology, Phycology & Bryology, Biostatistics

ecologist.publication: Farid, A., et al. (2023). Impact of Rhizospheric Nodulation and Soil Microbiome on Soybean and Rice Growth for Sustainable Agriculture. Asian Soil Journal.

projects.name: Threatened Flora Protection
projects.description: Successfully identified and demarcated populations of Grevillea juniperina and other threatened species prior to vegetation clearing, ensuring compliance and conservation.

projects.name: Weed Management & Habitat Restoration
projects.description: Reduced priority weed cover and restored degraded areas through targeted control, native planting and erosion control, improving habitat quality and biodiversity.

projects.name: Field Surveys & Data Collection
projects.description: Conducted flora surveys, vegetation assessments and habitat surveys across multiple sites using GPS, Survey123 and ArcGIS for accurate mapping and reporting.

projects.name: Client & Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description: Liaised with clients, land managers and site supervisors to communicate findings, provide recommendations and ensure successful delivery of ecological outcomes.

licences: Willing to obtain additional training (e.g. ChemCert, Spotter Catcher)

contact.travel: Willing to travel across Central West NSW
contact.vehicle: Class C Driver Licence · Own vehicle

footer.based: Based in Dubbo, NSW · Available for work across Central West NSW
footer.note: Self-motivated, safety-focused and passionate about ecology and conservation.
footer.motto: Protecting biodiversity. Restoring landscapes. Creating impact.
